name: Architext 1.7.0 Repository Intelligence
summary: Architext 1.7.0 is reserved for repository intelligence, diagram ergonomics, and persistent architecture annotations.
updateSource: 1.7.0 candidate capture (relocated from 1.6.0 when 1.6.0 became the routing overhaul)

planned.title: Repository tree view
planned.summary: Add a top-level Repo Tree tab that helps users understand source layout, optionally color-coding folders tied to C4 or Flow responsibilities and leaving room for a future 3D graph projection.

planned.title: Repository blast-radius search
planned.summary: Provide search for filenames, components, or known code concepts so users can see where a concern reaches across repository files and architecture elements.

planned.title: Diagram spacing controls
planned.summary: Add explicit ergonomics controls for diagram column width and sequence-diagram row spacing so users can improve readability without relying only on zoom.

planned.title: Persistent architecture element notes
planned.summary: Allow users to edit or annotate architecture elements and persist the notes in Architext JSON, including cases where a high-risk area is intentionally mitigated by the system being documented.

workstreams.name: Repository Intelligence
workstreams.summary: Expose repository structure and blast-radius understanding inside Architext.

workstreams.name: Diagram Ergonomics
workstreams.summary: Add layout controls that change diagram spacing without changing semantic data.

workstreams.name: Architecture Editing
workstreams.summary: Let users persist clarifying notes directly on architecture elements.
